expectations.text: Outputs contain a markdown file (CHANGES.md, audit.md, or similar) reporting the audit findings of the real wireframes folder.
expectations.evidence: CHANGES.md exists with full 8-step audit findings + proposed augmentation rationale + 'What was NOT changed' section

expectations.text: The audit confirms that operate-run.html, operate-iterate.html, and operate-build.html exist at the top level of the real wireframes folder.
expectations.evidence: Audit step 1 + step 8: 'Three canonical HTMLs (run / iterate / build, dated 2026-06-29). Top level: 3 HTMLs + README'

expectations.text: The audit confirms that historical/superseded files are quarantined under _historical/ (not mixed at top level).
expectations.evidence: Audit step 4: '_historical/ subfolder is real, contains exactly the 3 superseded files, and is named in the README. CLEAN.'

expectations.text: The audit identifies and names the machine-readable encoding file (lib/operate/mode-features.ts) as part of the triangulation.

expectations.text: The audit explicitly assesses whether the existing README's designer handoff section is sufficient for a cold-read designer.
expectations.evidence: Audit verdict: 'Folder is already handoff-ready against all 8 steps.' + 'Why augment despite cleanliness' section proposes specific improvement: designer's 2 scoped items are buried as items 1 and 9 of a 9-bullet list. Proposed PROPOSED-README.md restructures the brief.

expectations.text: The agent did NOT modify any files inside /Users/nplmini/code/work/systems/operating-sop/reference/wireframes/. All proposed changes live under the outputs/ directory.
expectations.evidence: Agent explicitly states 'I did NOT touch the real folder (constraint #3)'. Output files (CHANGES.md, PROPOSED-README.md) live under outputs/.
